name: navigate
description: Load the given URL in the active tab and wait for a page-load signal (Page.loadEventFired OR Page.frameStoppedLoading, whichever fires first). Falls back to document.readyState check before giving up.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait for load

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.close()"

name: click
description: Click the first element matching selector. Uses a single Runtime.evaluate call with getBoundingClientRect() — double-quoted JS delimiter so attribute selectors containing single quotes (e.g. a[href='/login']) work correctly. Scrolls element into view before measuring. Raises WebToolError if not found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.click('selector'); web.close()"

name: type
description: Type text into the element matched by selector.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.fill('selector', 'text'); web.close()"

name: screenshot
description: Take a JPEG screenshot and return the local file path (or raw base-64 if no filename given).
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.screenshot('screenshots/debug.jpg'); web.close()"
note: Use sparingly — only when DOM inspection is insufficient.

name: key
description: Press a keyboard key (Tab, Enter, Escape, ArrowDown, ArrowUp, etc.). Sends both keyDown and keyUp events.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.key('Tab'); web.close()"

name: execute_js
description: Evaluate JavaScript in the page context and return the result. Wrap objects/arrays in JSON.stringify().
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(web.js('document.title')); web.close()"

name: wait
description: Poll for a CSS selector at 0.1 s intervals; returns True as soon as the element appears, False on timeout.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); found = web.wait('.my-class', 15); print(found); web.close()"

name: wait_for_navigation
description: Block until the next page-load event fires (Page.loadEventFired or Page.frameStoppedLoading). Call this immediately after clicking a link or submit button to wait for the resulting navigation.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.click('a.nav-link'); web.wait_for_navigation(15); web.close()"

name: scroll
description: Scroll the page in a given direction by a number of pixels. Useful for revealing lazy-loaded content.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.scroll('down', 800); web.close()"

name: hover
description: Move the mouse over the first element matching selector to trigger :hover effects and reveal dropdown menus. Uses getBoundingClientRect() for reliable coordinates across all CSS3 pseudo-selectors including :first-child and :nth-child. Calls scrollIntoView() first.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.hover('nav .menu-item'); web.close()"

name: select_option
description: Set a <select> dropdown to the option with the given value attribute, then fire a change event.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.select_option('#country', 'US'); web.close()"

name: find_by_text
description: Find the first element whose visible text exactly matches the given string. Returns {tagName, selector} or null.

tag.description: Optional tag name to narrow the search (e.g. 'button', 'a', 'li')

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); info = web.find_by_text('Sign in', 'button'); print(info); web.close()"

name: click_by_text
description: Find the first element with the given visible text and click it. Raises WebToolError if not found.

tag.description: Optional tag name to narrow the search

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.click_by_text('Submit', 'button'); web.close()"

name: get_all_links
description: Return a list of all non-javascript: href URLs found on the current page. Single batched JS call.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); links = web.get_all_links(); print(json.dumps(links, indent=2)); web.close()"

name: get_table
description: Extract the first <table> matching selector as a list of dicts. Header cells become dict keys; each body row becomes one dict. Raises WebToolError if no table found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); rows = web.get_table('#myTable'); print(json.dumps(rows, indent=2)); web.close()"

name: get_form_fields
description: Introspect form structure. Returns a list of dicts (one per input/select/textarea) with keys: tag, type, name, id, value, placeholder, required.

selector.description: CSS selector for the form element

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); fields = web.get_form_fields('#login-form'); print(json.dumps(fields, indent=2)); web.close()"

name: open_tab
description: Open a new browser tab and return its tab_id string. Does NOT switch focus — call switch_tab(tab_id) to interact with the new tab.

url.description: Initial URL for the new tab

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); tid = web.open_tab('https://example.com'); web.switch_tab(tid); print(web.js('document.title')); web.close()"

name: switch_tab
description: Reconnect the WebSocket to the tab identified by tab_id. All subsequent commands operate on that tab.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); tid = web.open_tab('https://example.com'); web.switch_tab(tid); web.close()"

name: close_tab
description: Close the tab identified by tab_id. If it is the current tab, the WebSocket is also closed.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); tid = web.open_tab(); web.close_tab(tid); web.close()"

name: get_open_tabs
description: Return a list of dicts for every open page-type tab, each with keys: id, url, title.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(json.dumps(web.get_open_tabs(), indent=2)); web.close()"

name: coordinate_click
description: Click at specific x,y page coordinates. Fallback when CSS selectors cannot identify the target.
implementation: web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mousePressed', 'x': x, 'y': y, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1}); web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mouseReleased', 'x': x, 'y': y, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1})
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mousePressed', 'x': 400, 'y': 200, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1}); web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mouseReleased', 'x': 400, 'y': 200, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1}); web.close()"
when_to_use: When CSS selectors fail or JavaScript execution fails.

name: get_page_info
description: Return basic page metadata: URL, title, input count, button count.
implementation: web.js('JSON.stringify({url: window.location.href, title: document.title, inputCount: document.querySelectorAll("input").length, buttonCount: document.querySelectorAll("button").length})')
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(json.loads(web.js('JSON.stringify({url: window.location.href, title: document.title})'))); web.close()"

name: verify_completion
description: Check if task completed successfully by inspecting URL change and presence of success keywords in page text.
implementation: Compare web.js('window.location.href') with initial_url; search web.js('document.body.textContent.toLowerCase()') for success_keywords.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); final_url = web.js('window.location.href'); page_text = web.js('document.body.textContent.toLowerCase()'); success = any(k in page_text for k in ['success','complete','confirmed']); print(f'URL changed: {final_url != initial_url}, Success: {success}'); web.close()"

name: browser_pool
description: Create and manage an async pool of N independent headless Chrome processes. Supports 'async with' context manager and pool.map() for parallel task dispatch. Each slot owns a dedicated AsyncWebTool. Importable as 'from tools.web_tool import BrowserPool'.

size.description: Number of Chrome processes in the pool

implementation:
from tools.web_tool import BrowserPool

async with BrowserPool(size=2) as pool:
    results = await pool.map(async_fn, [url1, url2])
bash_usage:
python3 -c "
import asyncio
from tools.web_tool import BrowserPool, AsyncWebTool

async def fetch(web, url):
    await web.go_async(url)
    return await web.js_async('document.title')

async def main():
    async with BrowserPool(size=2) as pool:
        titles = await pool.map(fetch, ['https://example.com', 'https://quotes.toscrape.com'])
    print(titles)

asyncio.run(main())
"
map_signature: async def fn(web: AsyncWebTool, arg: Any) -> Any
note: Requires 'pip install websockets'. For sync usage, use tools.browser_pool.BrowserPool instead.

name: async_web_tool
description: Fully async CDP browser automation via AsyncWebTool (uses 'websockets' library). Provides go_async, cmd_async, js_async, click_async, fill_async, wait_async, screenshot_async. Used internally by BrowserPool.
implementation: from tools.web_tool import AsyncWebTool; web = AsyncWebTool(port=9222); await web.connect(); await web.go_async('URL'); title = await web.js_async('document.title'); await web.close()
bash_usage:
python3 -c "import asyncio; from tools.web_tool import AsyncWebTool
async def main():
    web = AsyncWebTool(9222)
    await web.connect()
    await web.go_async('https://example.com')
    print(await web.js_async('document.title'))
    await web.close()
asyncio.run(main())"

name: clone_website
description: Create a pixel-perfect website replica with all assets.
implementation: Follow clone/CLONING_PROTOCOL.md for systematic high-fidelity cloning.
bash_usage: Use WebTool + WebFetch + Write following the complete protocol.
note: Perfect replica cloning — not just content extraction.
